regression_risks: support/onboarding/release_export.sh does not actually export from HEAD: it uses git ls-files --cached --others --exclude-standard and copies files from the working tree with shutil.copy2, so dirty tracked edits and untracked unignored files can be shipped.
The release_export_exclusion pin only checks literal EXCLUDE_PATHS and printed follow-up commands; it does not detect the from-HEAD gap or accidental untracked-file inclusion.

support/checkers/check_project_declaration.py and support/operator/ledger_projection.py now skip any project/<id> directory with none of README.md, project.json, or buildings, not only an empty/status-only scaffold; arbitrary markerless project residue could become invisible to the checker and ledger.
The new project-declaration probe covers only project/brick-protocol/status/inbox, not a nonempty markerless project/<id> directory.
The documented dashboard bake command writes support/dashboard/public/dashboard-data.json; it was not executed here because this QA lane is read-only.
remaining_delta: Repair the export verb so the release tree is truly from HEAD, or fail closed on dirty tracked and untracked-unignored files before export; add a RED probe for dirty/untracked inclusion.
Narrow the project scaffold tolerance to the exact absent/empty/status-only case and add a RED probe for arbitrary markerless project/<id> residue.
Run the export verb in a writable temp dir, then in the exported tree verify no project/ exists, bake dashboard data, and run check_profile.py --all.
Run read_side_projection_boundary, product_no_smith_residue, release_export_exclusion, and the full gate in a writable temp source copy and record the temp paths.
